name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
ЩҒЩҺЩұШіЩ’ШӘЩҺЩҒЩ’ШӘЩҗЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’ ШЈЩҺЩ„ЩҗШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩғЩҺ ЩұЩ„Щ’ШЁЩҺЩҶЩҺШ§ШӘЩҸ ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…ЩҸ ЩұЩ„Щ’ШЁЩҺЩҶЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩӨЩ©)
```

```english
Ask them, are daughters to be for your Lord while sons are to be for them? (149)
```

```arabic
ШЈЩҺЩ…Щ’ Ш®ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩӮЩ’ЩҶЩҺШ§ ЩұЩ„Щ’Щ…ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҖЩ°Щ“ШҰЩҗЩғЩҺШ©ЩҺ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩҺЩҖЩ°Ш«ЩӢШ§ ЩҲЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШҙЩҺЩҖЩ°ЩҮЩҗШҜЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҘЩ )
```

```english
Did We create the angels females while they were present? (150)
```

```arabic
ШЈЩҺЩ„ЩҺШ§Щ“ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ… Щ…Щ‘ЩҗЩҶЩ’ ШҘЩҗЩҒЩ’ЩғЩҗЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’ Щ„ЩҺЩҠЩҺЩӮЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҘЩЎ)
```

```english
Be aware that it is out of their mendacity that they say, (151)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺШҜЩҺ ЩұЩ„Щ„Щ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸ ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ„ЩҺЩғЩҺЩҖЩ°Ш°ЩҗШЁЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҘЩў)
```

```english
вҖҳAllah has begotten [offsprings],вҖҷ and they indeed speak a falsehood. (152)
```

```arabic
ШЈЩҺШөЩ’Ш·ЩҺЩҒЩҺЩү ЩұЩ„Щ’ШЁЩҺЩҶЩҺШ§ШӘЩҗ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩү ЩұЩ„Щ’ШЁЩҺЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҘЩЈ)
```

```english
Has He preferred daughters to sons? (153)
```

```arabic
Щ…ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩғЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҒЩҺ ШӘЩҺШӯЩ’ЩғЩҸЩ…ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҘЩӨ)
```

```english
What is the matter with you? How do you judge? (154)
```

```arabic
ШЈЩҺЩҒЩҺЩ„ЩҺШ§ ШӘЩҺШ°ЩҺЩғЩ‘ЩҺШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҘЩҘ)
```

```english
Will you not then take admonition? (155)
```

```arabic
ШЈЩҺЩ…Щ’ Щ„ЩҺЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШіЩҸЩ„Щ’Ш·ЩҺЩҖЩ°ЩҶЩҢ Щ…Щ‘ЩҸШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҢ (ЩЎЩҘЩҰ)
```

```english
Do you have a manifest authority? (156)
```

```arabic
ЩҒЩҺШЈЩ’ШӘЩҸЩҲШ§Ыҹ ШЁЩҗЩғЩҗШӘЩҺЩҖЩ°ШЁЩҗЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШҘЩҗЩҶ ЩғЩҸЩҶШӘЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШөЩҺЩҖЩ°ШҜЩҗЩӮЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҘЩ§)
```

```english
Then produce your scripture, should you be truthful. (157)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺШ¬ЩҺШ№ЩҺЩ„ЩҸЩҲШ§Ыҹ ШЁЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҶЩҺЩҮЩҸЫҘ ЩҲЩҺШЁЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҶЩҺ ЩұЩ„Щ’Ш¬ЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ©Щҗ ЩҶЩҺШіЩҺШЁЩӢШ§ Ыҡ ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩӮЩҺШҜЩ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩ…ЩҺШӘЩҗ ЩұЩ„Щ’Ш¬ЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ©ЩҸ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҸШӯЩ’Ш¶ЩҺШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҘЩЁ)
```

```english
And they have set up a kinship between Him and the jinn, while the jinn certainly know that they will be presented [before Him]. (158)
```

```arabic
ШіЩҸШЁЩ’ШӯЩҺЩҖЩ°ЩҶЩҺ ЩұЩ„Щ„Щ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҗ Ш№ЩҺЩ…Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ЩҠЩҺШөЩҗЩҒЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҘЩ©)
```

```english
Clear is Allah of whatever they allege [about Him] (159)
```

```arabic
ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ Ш№ЩҗШЁЩҺШ§ШҜЩҺ ЩұЩ„Щ„Щ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҗ ЩұЩ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШ®Щ’Щ„ЩҺШөЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҰЩ )
```

```english
вҖ”[all] except AllahвҖҷs exclusive servants. (160)
```

```arabic
ЩҒЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҲЩҺЩ…ЩҺШ§ ШӘЩҺШ№Щ’ШЁЩҸШҜЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҰЩЎ)
```

```english
Indeed you and what you worship (161)
```

```arabic
Щ…ЩҺШ§Щ“ ШЈЩҺЩҶШӘЩҸЩ…Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҮЩҗ ШЁЩҗЩҒЩҺЩҖЩ°ШӘЩҗЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҰЩў)
```

```english
cannot mislead [anyone] about Him, (162)
```

```arabic
ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ…ЩҺЩҶЩ’ ЩҮЩҸЩҲЩҺ ШөЩҺШ§Щ„Щҗ ЩұЩ„Щ’Ш¬ЩҺШӯЩҗЩҠЩ…Щҗ (ЩЎЩҰЩЈ)
```

```english
except someone who is bound for hell. (163)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺЩ…ЩҺШ§ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§Щ“ ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸЫҘ Щ…ЩҺЩӮЩҺШ§Щ…ЩҢ Щ…Щ‘ЩҺШ№Щ’Щ„ЩҸЩҲЩ…ЩҢ (ЩЎЩҰЩӨ)
```

```english
вҖҳThere is none among us but has a known place. (164)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҶЩҺШӯЩ’ЩҶЩҸ ЩұЩ„ШөЩ‘ЩҺШ§Щ“ЩҒЩ‘ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҰЩҘ)
```

```english
It is we who are the ranged ones. (165)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҶЩҺШӯЩ’ЩҶЩҸ ЩұЩ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШіЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗШӯЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҰЩҰ)
```

```english
It is we who celebrate AllahвҖҷs glory.вҖҷ (166)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶ ЩғЩҺШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§Ыҹ Щ„ЩҺЩҠЩҺЩӮЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҰЩ§)
```

```english
They indeed, used to say, (167)
```

```arabic
Щ„ЩҺЩҲЩ’ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҺ Ш№ЩҗЩҶШҜЩҺЩҶЩҺШ§ Ш°ЩҗЩғЩ’ШұЩӢШ§ Щ…Щ‘ЩҗЩҶЩҺ ЩұЩ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩҲЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҰЩЁ)
```

```english
вҖҳHad we possessed a Reminder from our predecessors, (168)
```

```arabic
Щ„ЩҺЩғЩҸЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ Ш№ЩҗШЁЩҺШ§ШҜЩҺ ЩұЩ„Щ„Щ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҗ ЩұЩ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШ®Щ’Щ„ЩҺШөЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩҰЩ©)
```

```english
we would have surely been AllahвҖҷs exclusive servants.вҖҷ (169)
```

```arabic
ЩҒЩҺЩғЩҺЩҒЩҺШұЩҸЩҲШ§Ыҹ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗЫҰ Ы– ЩҒЩҺШіЩҺЩҲЩ’ЩҒЩҺ ЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩ§Щ )
```

```english
But they disbelieved it [when it came to them]. Soon they will know! (170)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩӮЩҺШҜЩ’ ШіЩҺШЁЩҺЩӮЩҺШӘЩ’ ЩғЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩ…ЩҺШӘЩҸЩҶЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҗШ№ЩҗШЁЩҺШ§ШҜЩҗЩҶЩҺШ§ ЩұЩ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШұЩ’ШіЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩ§ЩЎ)
```

```english
Certainly Our decree has gone beforehand in favour of Our servants, the apostles, (171)
```

```arabic
ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…ЩҸ ЩұЩ„Щ’Щ…ЩҺЩҶШөЩҸЩҲШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩ§Щў)
```

```english
that they will indeed receive [AllahвҖҷs] help, (172)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺ Ш¬ЩҸЩҶШҜЩҺЩҶЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…ЩҸ ЩұЩ„Щ’ШәЩҺЩҖЩ°Щ„ЩҗШЁЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩ§ЩЈ)
```

```english
and indeed Our hosts will be the victors. (173)
```

```arabic
ЩҒЩҺШӘЩҺЩҲЩҺЩ„Щ‘ЩҺ Ш№ЩҺЩҶЩ’ЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШӯЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺЩүЩ° ШӯЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҚ (ЩЎЩ§ЩӨ)
```

```english
So leave them alone for a while, (174)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺШЁЩ’ШөЩҗШұЩ’ЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҒЩҺШіЩҺЩҲЩ’ЩҒЩҺ ЩҠЩҸШЁЩ’ШөЩҗШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩ§ЩҘ)
```

```english
and watch them; soon they will see [the truth of the matter]! (175)
```

```arabic
ШЈЩҺЩҒЩҺШЁЩҗШ№ЩҺШ°ЩҺШ§ШЁЩҗЩҶЩҺШ§ ЩҠЩҺШіЩ’ШӘЩҺШ№Щ’Ш¬ЩҗЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩ§ЩҰ)
```

```english
Do they seek to hasten Our punishment? (176)
```

```arabic
ЩҒЩҺШҘЩҗШ°ЩҺШ§ ЩҶЩҺШІЩҺЩ„ЩҺ ШЁЩҗШіЩҺШ§ШӯЩҺШӘЩҗЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’ ЩҒЩҺШіЩҺШ§Щ“ШЎЩҺ ШөЩҺШЁЩҺШ§ШӯЩҸ ЩұЩ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸЩҶШ°ЩҺШұЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩ§Щ§)
```

```english
But when it descends in their courtyard it will be a dismal dawn for those who had been warned. (177)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺШӘЩҺЩҲЩҺЩ„Щ‘ЩҺ Ш№ЩҺЩҶЩ’ЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШӯЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺЩүЩ° ШӯЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҚ (ЩЎЩ§ЩЁ)
```

```english
So leave them alone for a while, (178)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺШЁЩ’ШөЩҗШұЩ’ ЩҒЩҺШіЩҺЩҲЩ’ЩҒЩҺ ЩҠЩҸШЁЩ’ШөЩҗШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩ§Щ©)
```

```english
and watch; soon they will see! (179)
```

```arabic
ШіЩҸШЁЩ’ШӯЩҺЩҖЩ°ЩҶЩҺ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩғЩҺ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘Щҗ ЩұЩ„Щ’Ш№ЩҗШІЩ‘ЩҺШ©Щҗ Ш№ЩҺЩ…Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ЩҠЩҺШөЩҗЩҒЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩЁЩ )
```

```english
Clear is your Lord, the Lord of Might, of whatever they allege [concerning Him]. (180)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺШіЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҖЩ°Щ…ЩҢ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩү ЩұЩ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШұЩ’ШіЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩЁЩЎ)
```

```english
Peace be to the apostles! (181)
```

```arabic
ЩҲЩҺЩұЩ„Щ’ШӯЩҺЩ…Щ’ШҜЩҸ Щ„ЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҗ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘Щҗ ЩұЩ„Щ’Ш№ЩҺЩҖЩ°Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ (ЩЎЩЁЩў)
```

```english
All praise belongs to Allah, Lord of all the worlds. (182)
```

## (Commentary)

The Glorified has previously presented that which clarifies that He is the Lord (Ш§Щ„ШұШЁ) вҖ” *the Sustainer and Master of all creation* вҖ” who is alone worthy of worship (Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш№ШЁЩҲШҜ), whom sincere servants (Ш№ШЁШ§ШҜ Щ…Ш®Щ„ШөЩҲЩҶ) like the honored prophets have worshipped, while others have disbelieved in Him; thus, He saved His servants and seized the disbelievers with a painful punishment. Then, in these verses, He addresses what they believe regarding their deitiesвҖ”namely the angels and the jinnвҖ”and their claim that the angels are the daughters of Allah and that there is a kinship (ЩҶШіШЁ) between Him and the jinn.

The Brahmanical and Buddhist idolaters and the Sabians did not claim femininity for all angels, though they did so for some. However, it is narrated from some tribes of the pagan Arabs, such as Juhaynah, Sulaym, Khuza'ah, and Banu Mulayh, that they claimed femininity for all angels. As for the jinn, the claim that their lineage traces back to Him in some manner is narrated from all of them.

In summary, the Exalted points out in these verses the corruption of their claims, then gives glad tidings of victory to the Prophet (ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ), threatens them with punishment, and concludes the Surah by hallowing Himself, sending peace upon the messengers, and praising Allah, the Lord of all the worlds (ШұШЁ Ш§Щ„Ш№Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҠЩҶ).

His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҒЩҺШ§ШіЩ’ШӘЩҺЩҒЩ’ШӘЩҗЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’ ШЈЩҺЩ„ЩҗШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩғЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЁЩҺЩҶШ§ШӘЩҸ ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЁЩҺЩҶЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Ask them for a pronouncement: Are daughters for your Lord while sons are for them?" (37:149). The Glorified has analyzed their claim that the angels are the daughters of Allah into its necessary implications (Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҲШ§ШІЩ…): that the angels are His children, that they are female, and that He, the Exalted, reserved daughters for Himself while they are reserved for sons. He then refuted these implications one by one. He refuted their claim that He has daughters while they have sons with His saying: **ЩҒЩҺШ§ШіЩ’ШӘЩҺЩҒЩ’ШӘЩҗЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’ ШЈЩҺЩ„ЩҗШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩғЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЁЩҺЩҶШ§ШӘЩҸ ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЁЩҺЩҶЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ**. This is a rhetorical question (Ш§ШіШӘЩҒЩҮШ§Щ… ШҘЩҶЩғШ§ШұЩҠ) denouncing their claim because of its implication that they are superior to Allah, since they favor sons over daughters, distance themselves from daughters, and bury them alive.

His saying, the Exalted: **ШЈЩҺЩ…Щ’ Ш®ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩӮЩ’ЩҶЩҺШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҺЩ„Ш§ШҰЩҗЩғЩҺШ©ЩҺ ШҘЩҗЩҶШ§Ш«Ш§ЩӢ ЩҲЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШҙШ§ЩҮЩҗШҜЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Did We create the angels female while they were witnesses?" (37:150). The word "or" (ШЈЩҺЩ…Щ’) is disjunctive (Щ…ЩҶЩӮШ·Ш№Ш©), meaning: "Nay, did We create the angels female while they were witnesses?"вҖ”that is, witnessing their creation? They were not witnesses to their creation, nor do they have the right to claim such a thing. Masculinity and femininity are matters that cannot be established except through some form of sensory perception (Ш§Щ„ШӯШі). This is a refutation of their claim regarding the femininity of the angels.

His saying, the Exalted: **ШЈЩҺЩ„Ш§ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ’ ШҘЩҗЩҒЩ’ЩғЩҗЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’ Щ„ЩҺЩҠЩҺЩӮЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺШҜЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҸ ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ„ЩҺЩғШ§Ш°ЩҗШЁЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Look! It is indeed out of their mendacity that they say, 'Allah has begotten,' and they are indeed liars." (37:151-152). This is a refutation of their claim of procreation by stating it is part of their mendacity (Ш§Щ„ШҘЩҒЩғ) вҖ” *the turning of speech away from its proper course toward another* вҖ” i.e., from truth to falsehood. They characterize the creation of the angels as what they consider procreation and express it as such; thus, they are fabricators and liars.

His saying, the Exalted: **ШЈЩҺШөЩ’Ш·ЩҺЩҒЩҺЩү Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЁЩҺЩҶШ§ШӘЩҸ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩү Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЁЩҺЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ Щ…Ш§ Щ„ЩҺЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩғЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҒЩҺ ШӘЩҺШӯЩ’ЩғЩҸЩ…ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ ШЈЩҺЩҒЩҺЩ„Ш§ ШӘЩҺШ°ЩҺЩғЩ‘ЩҺШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Has He preferred daughters to sons? What is the matter with you? How do you judge? Will you not then take admonition?" (37:153-155).

He repeated the denunciation of the "choosing of daughters" from among the implications of their claim due to its extreme loathsomeness.

Then He rebuked them with His saying: **Щ…Ш§ Щ„ЩҺЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩғЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҒЩҺ ШӘЩҺШӯЩ’ЩғЩҸЩ…ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "What is the matter with you? How do you judge?" because their claim is a judgment without evidence. He followed it with His saying: **ШЈЩҺЩҒЩҺЩ„Ш§ ШӘЩҺШ°ЩҺЩғЩ‘ЩҺШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Will you not then take admonition?" as a rebuke and an indication that their claimвҖ”besides being without evidenceвҖ”is contradicted by evidence itself. Had they reflected, it would have been revealed to them that His sanctuary, the Exalted, is hallowed (ШӘЩҶШІЩҮШӘ) above being partitioned so as to give birth, or being in need so as to take a child. He has argued against them with this in various places of His speech.

The shift (Ш§Щ„Ш§Щ„ШӘЩҒШ§ШӘ) from the third person to the second person (address) is to indicate the intensity of wrath (Ш§Щ„ШіШ®Ш·) which necessitates rebuking them directly.

His saying, the Exalted: **ШЈЩҺЩ…Щ’ Щ„ЩҺЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШіЩҸЩ„Щ’Ш·Ш§ЩҶЩҢ Щ…ЩҸШЁЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҢ ЩҒЩҺШЈЩ’ШӘЩҸЩҲШ§ ШЁЩҗЩғЩҗШӘШ§ШЁЩҗЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ’ ЩғЩҸЩҶЩ’ШӘЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШөШ§ШҜЩҗЩӮЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Do you have a manifest authority? Then bring your scripture, if you are truthful." (37:156-157). The word "or" (ШЈЩҺЩ…Щ’) is disjunctive. What is meant by "authority" (Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш·Ш§ЩҶ) вҖ” *the proof or demonstration* вҖ” is a scripture revealed from Allah, the Glorified, informing that the angels are His daughters, as suggested by the context. Since this was not established by intellect (Ш№ЩӮЩ„) or sense (ШӯШі), it remained to be established by a scripture from Allah revealed through revelation (Ш§Щ„ЩҲШӯЩҠ). If their claim were true and they were truthful in it, they would be able to produce the scripture.

The attribution of the scripture to them (ШЁЩҗЩғЩҗШӘШ§ШЁЩҗЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’) is based on the assumption of its existence as a proof for their claim.

His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺШ¬ЩҺШ№ЩҺЩ„ЩҸЩҲШ§ ШЁЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҶЩҺЩҮЩҸ ЩҲЩҺШЁЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш¬ЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ©Щҗ ЩҶЩҺШіЩҺШЁШ§ЩӢ ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩӮЩҺШҜЩ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩ…ЩҺШӘЩҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш¬ЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ©ЩҸ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҸШӯЩ’Ш¶ЩҺШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "And they have set up a kinship between Him and the jinn, while the jinn certainly know that they will indeed be hauled [for judgment]." (37:158). Setting up a kinship between Him and the jinn refers to their claim that the jinn are His offspring. The details of their claims were previously discussed in the commentary on Surah Hud regarding the worship of idols.

His saying: **ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩӮЩҺШҜЩ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩ…ЩҺШӘЩҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш¬ЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ©ЩҸ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҸШӯЩ’Ш¶ЩҺШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "while the jinn certainly know that they will indeed be hauled [for judgment]" means for the reckoning or for the Fire, as suggested by the absolute use of "hauled" (Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҸШӯЩ’Ш¶ЩҺШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ). In any case, they know that they are subjects (Щ…ШұШЁЩҲШЁЩҲЩҶ) of Allah, who will reckon them and recompense them for what they have done. Thus, between them and Allah, the Glorified, there is the relationship of Lordship (Ш§Щ„ШұШЁЩҲШЁЩҠШ©) and servitude (Ш§Щ„Ш№ШЁЩҲШҜЩҠШ©), not the kinship of procreation. Whoever is such does not deserve worship.

It is strange that some have said the "jinn" (Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©) here refers to a group of angels called by that name, which would necessitate returning the pronoun in "they" (ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’) to the disbelievers rather than the jinn. This is something for which there is no evidence in His speech, the Exalted, in addition to its distance from the context.

His saying, the Exalted: **ШіЩҸШЁЩ’ШӯШ§ЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҗ Ш№ЩҺЩ…Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ЩҠЩҺШөЩҗЩҒЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ Ш№ЩҗШЁШ§ШҜЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШ®Щ’Щ„ЩҺШөЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Immune is Allah from what they describe, except for the chosen servants of Allah." (37:159-160). The pronoun in "they describe" (ЩҠЩҺШөЩҗЩҒЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ)вҖ”considering the connection of the verse to what precedes itвҖ”refers to the aforementioned disbelievers. The exception (Ш§Щ„Ш§ШіШӘШ«ЩҶШ§ШЎ) is disjunctive (Щ…ЩҶЩӮШ·Ш№). The meaning is: He is hallowed above their descriptionвҖ”or above the attributes the disbelievers describe Him with, such as procreation, kinship, partnership, and the likeвҖ”but the sincere servants of Allah (Ш№ШЁШ§ШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш®Щ„ШөЩҠЩҶ) describe Him with a description that befits HimвҖ”or with attributes that are worthy of Him.

It has been said that it is a disjunctive exception from the pronoun in "hauled" (Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҸШӯЩ’Ш¶ЩҺШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ), and it has been said it is from the subject of "they have set up" (Ш¬ЩҺШ№ЩҺЩ„ЩҸЩҲШ§), with the intervening sentences being a parenthetical insertion. Both of these views are far-fetched.

However, the two verses, when taken independently, possess a broader and more subtle meaning: that the pronoun in "they describe" (ЩҠЩҺШөЩҗЩҒЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ) refers to people in general, and the "description" (Ш§Щ„ЩҲШөЩҒ) is absolute, encompassing everything any describer attributes to Him. The exception is then continuous (Щ…ШӘШөЩ„), and the meaning is: He is hallowed above everything that describers attribute to Him, except for the sincere servants of Allah.

This is because people only describe Him using limited concepts (Щ…ЩҒШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… Щ…ШӯШҜЩҲШҜШ©) they possess, while He, the Glorified, is unlimited; no boundary encompasses Him, and no attribute perceives Him. Everything He is described with, He is more majestic than it; and everything imagined to be Him, He is other than it. However, the Glorified has servants whom He has purified for Himself and reserved for Himself, such that no one else shares in them. He has made Himself known to them and made them forget everything else; they know Him, and they know others through Him. Thus, when they describe Him within their souls, they describe Him with what befits the sanctuary of His Greatness (ЩғШЁШұЩҠШ§ШҰЩҮ); and when they describe Him with their tonguesвҖ”while words are deficient and meanings are limitedвҖ”they admit the inadequacy of expression and acknowledge the fatigue of the tongue.

As the Prophet (ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ) saidвҖ”and he is the master of the sincere: **Щ„Ш§ ШЈШӯШөЩҠ Ш«ЩҶШ§ШЎ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩғ ШЈЩҶШӘ ЩғЩ…Ш§ ШЈШ«ЩҶЩҠШӘ Ш№Щ„Щү ЩҶЩҒШіЩғ** вҖ” "I cannot enumerate Your praise; You are as You have praised Yourself." Understand this.

His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҒШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩғЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ШӘЩҺШ№Щ’ШЁЩҸШҜЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Щ…Ш§ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’ШӘЩҸЩ…Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҮЩҗ ШЁЩҗЩҒШ§ШӘЩҗЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ…ЩҺЩҶЩ’ ЩҮЩҸЩҲЩҺ ШөШ§Щ„Щҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш¬ЩҺШӯЩҗЩҠЩ…Щҗ** вҖ” "For indeed you and what you worship, you cannot beguile [anyone] against Him, except him who is to roast in hell." (37:161-163). This is a derivation (ШӘЩҒШұЩҠШ№) from the ruling of the excepted and the excepted-from, or the excepted specifically. The meaning is: since what you have described is misguidanceвҖ”and the sincere servants of Allah are not misguided in their descriptionвҖ”you are not misguiding anyone by it except those who tread the path of the Fire.

It is apparent from the context that "what" (Щ…Ш§) in "what you worship" (Щ…Ш§ ШӘЩҺШ№Щ’ШЁЩҸШҜЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ) is a relative pronoun (Щ…ЩҲШөЩҲЩ„Ш©) referring to the idols alone, or the idols and the deities of misguidance like the devils of the jinn. The "not" (Щ…Ш§) in "you are not" (Щ…Ш§ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’ШӘЩҸЩ…Щ’) is negating (ЩҶШ§ЩҒЩҠШ©). The pronoun in "against Him" (Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҮЩҗ) refers to Allah, the Glorified, and the prepositional phrase is connected to "beguiling" (ШЁЩҒШ§ШӘЩҶЩҠЩҶ). "Beguiling" (ЩҒШ§ШӘЩҶЩҠЩҶ) is the active participle of "fitnah" (Ш§Щ„ЩҒШӘЩҶШ©) in the sense of misguiding (Ш§Щ„ШҘШ¶Щ„Ш§Щ„). "Roasting" (ШөШ§Щ„Щҗ) is from "salw" (Ш§Щ„ШөЩ„ЩҲ) in the sense of following (Ш§Щ„Ш§ШӘШЁШ§Ш№); thus, the "roaster of hell" (ШөШ§Щ„ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ШӯЩҠЩ…) is the follower of hell who treads the path of the Fire. The exception is elliptical (Щ…ЩҒШұШә), its estimation being: "you cannot beguile anyone except him who is to roast in hell."

The meaning is: indeed you and the deities of misguidance that you worship, all of you together cannot misguide anyone against Allah except him who is a follower of hell.

It has been said that the first "what" (Щ…Ш§) is infinitive (Щ…ШөШҜШұЩҠШ©) or relative, and the sentence "For indeed you and what you worship" is a complete, independent statement of the type "you and your business" (ШЈЩҶШӘ ЩҲШҙШЈЩҶЩғ), meaning: "you and what you worship are paired." Then a new sentence begins. It has also been said that "you cannot beguile" (Щ…Ш§ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’ШӘЩҸЩ…Щ’ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҮЩҗ ШЁЩҗЩҒШ§ШӘЩҗЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ) carries the meaning of "compelling" (Ш§Щ„ШӯЩ…Щ„), and the pronoun in "against it" (Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҮЩҗ) refers to "what you worship" (Щ…Ш§ ШӘЩҺШ№Щ’ШЁЩҸШҜЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ) if "what" is infinitive, or to "what" with an estimated mudaf if it is relative. The meaning would be: "you cannot compel [anyone] to your worship or to the worship of what you worship except him who is to roast in hell."

It was also said that "against" (Ш№Щ„Щү) could mean "by" (Ш§Щ„ШЁШ§ШЎ), and the pronoun refers to "what you worship" or to "what" if it were relative, with "beguiling" (ШЁЩҒШ§ШӘЩҶЩҠЩҶ) remaining in its literal sense without implied meaning. The meaning would be: "you cannot misguide anyone by your worship or by the worship of what you worship except..."

All of these are forced interpretations (ШӘЩғЩ„ЩҒШ§ШӘ) without necessity. The discussion regarding the shift (Ш§Щ„Ш§Щ„ШӘЩҒШ§ШӘ) in this verse is the same as the discussion regarding it previously.

His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸ Щ…ЩҺЩӮШ§Щ…ЩҢ Щ…ЩҺШ№Щ’Щ„ЩҸЩҲЩ…ЩҢ ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҶЩҺШӯЩ’ЩҶЩҸ Ш§Щ„ШөЩ‘ЩҺШ§ЩҒЩ‘ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҶЩҺШӯЩ’ЩҶЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШіЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗШӯЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "There is none of us but has a known station. Indeed we are those who stand in ranks. Indeed we are those who celebrate [His] glory." (37:164-166). These three versesвҖ”as indicated by the contextвҖ”are a parenthetical interjection (Ш§Ш№ШӘШұШ§Ш¶) from the speech of Gabriel or him and his assistants from the angels of revelation (Щ…Щ„Ш§ШҰЩғШ© Ш§Щ„ЩҲШӯЩҠ), similar to His saying, the Exalted, in Surah Maryam: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ЩҶЩҺШӘЩҺЩҶЩҺШІЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҸ ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ШЁЩҗШЈЩҺЩ…Щ’ШұЩҗ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩғЩҺ Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸ Щ…Ш§ ШЁЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҶЩҺ ШЈЩҺЩҠЩ’ШҜЩҗЩҠЩҶШ§ ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ Ш®ЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҒЩҺЩҶШ§ ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ШЁЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҶЩҺ Ш°Щ„ЩҗЩғЩҺ** вҖ” "We do not descend except by the command of your Lord. To Him belongs whatever is before us and whatever is behind us and whatever is in between..." (19:64).

It has been said that they are from the speech of the Messenger (ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ) describing himself and the believers to the disbelievers as a rebuke and reproach to them, and it is connected to His saying: "Ask them for a pronouncement" (ЩҒЩҺШ§ШіЩ’ШӘЩҺЩҒЩ’ШӘЩҗЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’). The estimation would be: "Ask them and say: there is none of us, the community of Muslims, but has a known station according to his deeds on the Day of Resurrection; and indeed we are those who stand in ranks in prayer, and indeed we are those who celebrate [His] glory." This is a forced interpretation that does not suit the context.

The three verses are intended to refute their claim of the divinity of the angels by citing the angels' own admission of that which negates the disbelievers' claim. They do not deny servitude (Ш§Щ„Ш№ШЁЩҲШҜЩҠШ©) for the angels; rather, they believe that they are subjects (Щ…ШұШЁЩҲШЁЩҲЩҶ) of Allah, yet lords (ШЈШұШЁШ§ШЁ) and deities (ШўЩ„ЩҮШ©) for those below them, acting independently in what has been delegated to them of the world's affairs without any of this management being linked to Allah, the Glorified. This is what the angels negate for themselvesвҖ”not their being intermediary causes (ШЈШіШЁШ§ШЁ Щ…ШӘЩҲШіШ·Ш©) between Him, the Exalted, and His creation, as He, the Exalted, said: **ШЁЩҺЩ„Щ’ Ш№ЩҗШЁШ§ШҜЩҢ Щ…ЩҸЩғЩ’ШұЩҺЩ…ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҺШіЩ’ШЁЩҗЩӮЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺЩҮЩҸ ШЁЩҗШ§Щ„Щ’ЩӮЩҺЩҲЩ’Щ„Щҗ ЩҲЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШЁЩҗШЈЩҺЩ…Щ’ШұЩҗЩҮЩҗ ЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ…ЩҺЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Rather they are [His] honored servants. They do not venture to speak ahead of Him, and they act by His command." (21:26-27).

Thus, His saying: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸ Щ…ЩҺЩӮШ§Щ…ЩҢ Щ…ЩҺШ№Щ’Щ„ЩҸЩҲЩ…ЩҢ** вҖ” "There is none of us but has a known station" means a designated, specific station in which he has been placed, which he cannot exceed by having an affair delegated to him so that he acts independently in it. Rather, he is naturally disposed (Щ…Ш¬ШЁЩҲЩ„) toward obedience to Allah in what He commands and toward His worship.

His saying: **ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҶЩҺШӯЩ’ЩҶЩҸ Ш§Щ„ШөЩ‘ЩҺШ§ЩҒЩ‘ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Indeed we are those who stand in ranks" means we stand in ranks before Allah awaiting His commands in the management of the world to carry them out as He wills. As He, the Exalted, said: **Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’ШөЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҺ Щ…Ш§ ШЈЩҺЩ…ЩҺШұЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҲЩҺЩҠЩҺЩҒЩ’Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Щ…Ш§ ЩҠЩҸШӨЩ’Щ…ЩҺШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "they do not disobey Allah in what He commands them, and they carry out what they are commanded" (66:6). This is what the context suggests. It is sometimes said that the meaning is "we stand in ranks for prayer before Allah," but this is far from the understanding and has no evidence.

His saying: **ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҶЩҺШӯЩ’ЩҶЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШіЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗШӯЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Indeed we are those who celebrate [His] glory" means those who hallow Him, the Exalted, from what does not befit the sanctuary of His Greatness. As He, the Exalted, said: **ЩҠЩҸШіЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗШӯЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ„Щ‘ЩҺЩҠЩ’Щ„ЩҺ ЩҲЩҺШ§Щ„ЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮШ§ШұЩҺ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҺЩҒЩ’ШӘЩҸШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "They celebrate [His] glory night and day, and they do not flag." (21:20).

Thus, the three verses describe the position of the angels in creation and their work appropriate to their creation, which is standing in ranks to receive His command, the Exalted, and hallowing the sanctuary of His Greatness from any partner and everything that does not befit the perfection of His transcendent Essence.

His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ’ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҠЩҺЩӮЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ Щ„ЩҺЩҲЩ’ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҺ Ш№ЩҗЩҶЩ’ШҜЩҺЩҶШ§ Ш°ЩҗЩғЩ’ШұШ§ЩӢ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩҲЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ Щ„ЩҺЩғЩҸЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ Ш№ЩҗШЁШ§ШҜЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШ®Щ’Щ„ЩҺШөЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Indeed they used to say, 'Had we a reminder from the ancients, we would have surely been AllahвҖҷs chosen servants.'" (37:167-169). This is a return to the previous context.

The pronoun in His saying: "Indeed they used to say" (ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ’ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҠЩҺЩӮЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ) refers to the Quraysh and those who follow them. The "if" (ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ’) is the lightened form of the heavy (Щ…Ш®ЩҒЩҒШ© Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш«ЩӮЩҠЩ„Ш©). What is meant by "a reminder from the ancients" (Ш°ЩҗЩғЩ’ШұШ§ЩӢ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺЩҲЩ‘ЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ) is a heavenly scripture of the same kind as the scriptures revealed to the ancients.

The meaning is: "Had we a heavenly scripture of the kind revealed before us to the ancients, we would have been guided and would have been the sincere servants of Allah." They mean that they are excused if they disbelieve because the proof (Ш§Щ„ШӯШ¬Ш©) has not been established against them by Allah, the Glorified.

This is, in reality, a lapse (ЩҮЩҒЩҲШ©) on their part, for the creed of idolatry (Ш§Щ„ЩҲШ«ЩҶЩҠШ©) deems prophethood (Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҲШ©), messengership (Ш§Щ„ШұШіШ§Щ„Ш©), and the descent of a heavenly scripture to be impossible.

His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҒЩҺЩғЩҺЩҒЩҺШұЩҸЩҲШ§ ШЁЩҗЩҮЩҗ ЩҒЩҺШіЩҺЩҲЩ’ЩҒЩҺ ЩҠЩҺШ№Щ’Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "But they disbelieved in it; so soon they will know." (37:170). The "so" (Ш§Щ„ЩҒШ§ШЎ) is elided (ЩҒШөЩҠШӯШ©). The meaning is: "So We revealed to them the Reminder (Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩғШұ), which is the Noble Quran, but they disbelieved in it and did not fulfill what they said; so soon they will know the evil consequence of their disbelief."

This is a threat from Him, the Exalted, to them.

His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩӮЩҺШҜЩ’ ШіЩҺШЁЩҺЩӮЩҺШӘЩ’ ЩғЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩ…ЩҺШӘЩҸЩҶШ§ Щ„ЩҗШ№ЩҗШЁШ§ШҜЩҗЩҶЩҺШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШұЩ’ШіЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҺЩҶЩ’ШөЩҸЩҲШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Certainly Our decree has been passed for Our servants, the apostles, that they will indeed be the ones to be helped" (37:171-172). His "decree" (ЩғЩ„Щ…ШӘЩҮ) for them is His word that He spoke regarding them, which is His judgment and ordinance (ЩӮШ¶Ш§ШӨЩҮ) concerning them. The "precedence" (ШіШЁЩӮ) of the decree means its priority in time or its priority in effectiveness and dominance. The preposition "for" (Ш§Щ„Щ„Ш§Щ…) conveys the meaning of benefit; i.e., "We have decreed a definitive judgment regarding them: that they are the ones who are helped." The speech has been emphasized with various forms of emphasis.

The "help" or "victory" (Ш§Щ„ЩҶШөШұ) has been left absolute without being restricted to this world or the hereafter, or in any other way. Rather, the evidence is to the contrary; He, the Exalted, said: **ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҶЩҺЩҶЩ’ШөЩҸШұЩҸ ШұЩҸШіЩҸЩ„ЩҺЩҶШ§ ЩҲЩҺШ§Щ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШўЩ…ЩҺЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҒЩҗЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШӯЩҺЩҠШ§Ш©Щҗ Ш§Щ„ШҜЩ‘ЩҸЩҶЩ’ЩҠШ§ ЩҲЩҺЩҠЩҺЩҲЩ’Щ…ЩҺ ЩҠЩҺЩӮЩҸЩҲЩ…ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺШҙЩ’ЩҮШ§ШҜЩҸ** вҖ” "Indeed We shall help Our apostles and those who have faith in the life of the world and on the day when the witnesses rise up" (40:51).

Thus, the messengers (peace be upon them) are helped in argument because they are upon the truth, and the truth is never defeated.

They are helped against their enemies, either by making them prevail over them or by taking vengeance upon them. He, the Exalted, said: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…Ш§ ШЈЩҺШұЩ’ШіЩҺЩ„Щ’ЩҶШ§ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ’ ЩӮЩҺШЁЩ’Щ„ЩҗЩғЩҺ ШҘЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ШұЩҗШ¬Ш§Щ„Ш§ЩӢ ЩҶЩҸЩҲШӯЩҗЩҠ ШҘЩҗЩ„ЩҺЩҠЩ’ЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’ Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩ’ ШЈЩҺЩҮЩ’Щ„Щҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’ЩӮЩҸШұЩү** вҖ” until He said вҖ” **ШӯЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺЩү ШҘЩҗШ°ЩҺШ§ Ш§ШіЩ’ШӘЩҺЩҠЩ’ШЈЩҺШіЩҺ Ш§Щ„ШұЩ‘ЩҸШіЩҸЩ„ЩҸ ЩҲЩҺШёЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҸЩҲШ§ ШЈЩҺЩҶЩ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩӮЩҺШҜЩ’ ЩғЩҸШ°ЩҗШЁЩҸЩҲШ§ Ш¬Ш§ШЎЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҶЩҺШөЩ’ШұЩҸЩҶШ§ ЩҒЩҺЩҶЩҸШ¬Щ‘ЩҗЩҠЩҺ Щ…ЩҺЩҶЩ’ ЩҶЩҺШҙШ§ШЎЩҸ ЩҲЩҺЩ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҸШұЩҺШҜЩ‘ЩҸ ШЁЩҺШЈЩ’ШіЩҸЩҶШ§ Ш№ЩҺЩҶЩҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’ЩӮЩҺЩҲЩ’Щ…Щҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШ¬Щ’ШұЩҗЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "We did not send [any] before you except men from the people of the towns, to whom We revealed... until when the apostles lost hope and thought they had been called liars, Our help came to them; then We would deliver whomever We wished, and Our punishment is not averted from the guilty lot." (12:109-110).

They are helped in the hereafter, as He, the Exalted, said: **ЩҠЩҺЩҲЩ’Щ…ЩҺ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҸШ®Щ’ШІЩҗЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҸ Ш§Щ„ЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШЁЩҗЩҠЩ‘ЩҺ ЩҲЩҺШ§Щ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШўЩ…ЩҺЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ Щ…ЩҺШ№ЩҺЩҮЩҸ** вҖ” "the day Allah will not let the Prophet down and those who have faith with him" (66:8). A verse in Surah al-Mu'min (Ghafir) was just mentioned with this meaning.

His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺ Ш¬ЩҸЩҶЩ’ШҜЩҺЩҶШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸЩ…ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШәШ§Щ„ЩҗШЁЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "and that Our host will indeed be the victors." (37:173). A "host" (Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШҜ) is a dense gathering, which is why it is used for an army; thus, it is close in meaning to "party" (Ш§Щ„ШӯШІШЁ). He, the Exalted, said in another place of His speech: **ЩҲЩҺЩ…ЩҺЩҶЩ’ ЩҠЩҺШӘЩҺЩҲЩҺЩ„Щ‘ЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҺ ЩҲЩҺШұЩҺШіЩҸЩҲЩ„ЩҺЩҮЩҸ ЩҲЩҺШ§Щ„Щ‘ЩҺШ°ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ ШўЩ…ЩҺЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҒЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺ ШӯЩҗШІЩ’ШЁЩҺ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩҗ ЩҮЩҸЩ…ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШәШ§Щ„ЩҗШЁЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Whoever takes for his guardians Allah, His Apostle and the faithful [should know that] the confederates of Allah are indeed the victors." (5:56).

What is meant by "Our host" (Ш¬ЩҸЩҶЩ’ШҜЩҺЩҶШ§) is the community that obeys His command and strives in His way, and they are the believers specifically, or the prophets and those who follow them from the believers. In the second interpretation, there is a generalization after specialization. In any case, the believers are helped just like those they follow among the prophets. He, the Exalted, said: **ЩҲЩҺЩ„Ш§ ШӘЩҺЩҮЩҗЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҲЩҺЩ„Ш§ ШӘЩҺШӯЩ’ШІЩҺЩҶЩҸЩҲШ§ ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺЩҶЩ’ШӘЩҸЩ…ЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’ШЈЩҺШ№Щ’Щ„ЩҺЩҲЩ’ЩҶЩҺ ШҘЩҗЩҶЩ’ ЩғЩҸЩҶЩ’ШӘЩҸЩ…Щ’ Щ…ЩҸШӨЩ’Щ…ЩҗЩҶЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Do not overtax yourselves and do not grieve, and you shall be the upper ones, should you be faithful." (3:139). Some verses indicating this were just mentioned.

The rulingвҖ”namely help and victoryвҖ”is a social ruling (ШӯЩғЩ… Ш§Ш¬ШӘЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠ) contingent upon the title and nothing else. That is, the messengers, who are servants Allah has sent, and the believers, who are a host for Allah acting by His command and striving in His way, as long as they remain upon this description, are helped and victorious. However, if nothing remains of faith but its name, and nothing of the affiliation but a story, then neither help nor victory should be expected.

His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҒЩҺШӘЩҺЩҲЩҺЩ„Щ‘ЩҺ Ш№ЩҺЩҶЩ’ЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШӯЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺЩү ШӯЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҚ** вҖ” "So leave them alone for a while" (37:174). This is a derivation (ШӘЩҒШұЩҠШ№) from the discourse on help and victory. In it is a promise to the Prophet (ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ) of help and victory, and a threat to the polytheists and to the Quraysh specifically.

The command to turn away from them, then making it limited by His saying: "for a while" (ШӯЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺЩү ШӯЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҚ), hints that the term is not far off. And so it was; the Prophet (ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ) emigrated shortly thereafter, and Allah destroyed the leaders of the Quraysh in the Battle of Badr and others.

His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺШЁЩ’ШөЩҗШұЩ’ЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ЩҒЩҺШіЩҺЩҲЩ’ЩҒЩҺ ЩҠЩҸШЁЩ’ШөЩҗШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "and watch them, for soon they will see." (37:175). The command to watch and the informing of their seeing soon, and the conjunction of the speech with the command to turn away immediately, impliesвҖ”according to the comparisonвҖ”that the meaning is: "Observe them and see the denial and stubbornness they are upon in the face of your warning and frightening, for soon they will see the evil consequence of their denial and arrogance."

His saying, the Exalted: **ШЈЩҺЩҒЩҺШЁЩҗШ№ЩҺШ°Ш§ШЁЩҗЩҶШ§ ЩҠЩҺШіЩ’ШӘЩҺШ№Щ’Ш¬ЩҗЩ„ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ ЩҒЩҺШҘЩҗШ°Ш§ ЩҶЩҺШІЩҺЩ„ЩҺ ШЁЩҗШіШ§ШӯЩҺШӘЩҗЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’ ЩҒЩҺШіШ§ШЎЩҺ ШөЩҺШЁШ§ШӯЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҺШұЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Do they seek to hasten Our punishment? But when it descends in their courtyard, evil will be the morning of those who were warned." (37:176-177). This is a rebuke to them for their seeking to hasten and their saying: "When is this promise? When is this victory?" It is an announcement that this punishment is not something that should be hastened, for it results in a miserable day and an ill-omened morning.

The "descending of the punishment in their courtyard" (ШЁЩҗШіШ§ШӯЩҺШӘЩҗЩҮЩҗЩ…Щ’) is a metaphor for its descending upon them in a manner of encompassment and surrounding. His saying: **ЩҒЩҺШіШ§ШЎЩҺ ШөЩҺШЁШ§ШӯЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸЩҶЩ’Ш°ЩҺШұЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "evil will be the morning of those who were warned" means: "how wretched is their morning as a morning." The "warned" (Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҶШ°ШұЩҲЩҶ) are the polytheists of the Quraysh.

His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺШӘЩҺЩҲЩҺЩ„Щ‘ЩҺ Ш№ЩҺЩҶЩ’ЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’ ШӯЩҺШӘЩ‘ЩҺЩү ШӯЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҚ ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺШЁЩ’ШөЩҗШұЩ’ ЩҒЩҺШіЩҺЩҲЩ’ЩҒЩҺ ЩҠЩҸШЁЩ’ШөЩҗШұЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "And leave them alone for a while, and watch, for soon they will see." (37:178-179). This is an emphasis of what has passed by repeating the two verses, as has been said. Some have suggested that what preceded was a threat of the punishment of this world, and this is a threat of the punishment of the hereafter. This is not without merit, for in this verse "and watch" (ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺШЁЩ’ШөЩҗШұЩ’) occurs without an object, unlike the previous verse "and watch them" (ЩҲЩҺШЈЩҺШЁЩ’ШөЩҗШұЩ’ЩҮЩҸЩ…Щ’). The omission suggests generalityвҖ”that the meaning is to watch what the generality of people are upon of disbelief and transgressionвҖ”and this is suited to the threat of the punishment of the Day of Resurrection.

His saying, the Exalted: **ШіЩҸШЁЩ’ШӯШ§ЩҶЩҺ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩғЩҺ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘Щҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш№ЩҗШІЩ‘ЩҺШ©Щҗ Ш№ЩҺЩ…Щ‘ЩҺШ§ ЩҠЩҺШөЩҗЩҒЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Immune is your Lord, the Lord of Might, from what they describe." (37:180). This is a hallowing of Him, the Exalted, from what the disbelieversвҖ”who oppose the call of the Prophet (ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ)вҖ”describe Him with, as previously mentioned in the Surah.

The proof for this is the attribution of the hallowing to His saying: "your Lord" (ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗЩғЩҺ)вҖ”i.e., the Lord whom you worship and to whom you callвҖ”and the attribution of "Lord" a second time to "Might" (Ш§Щ„Ш№ШІШ©), which implies His exclusive possession, the Exalted, of Might. Thus, His side is absolutely impregnable; no humiliator can humiliate Him, no victor can defeat Him, and no fleer can escape Him. Therefore, the polytheists, the enemies of the truth who are threatened with punishment, are not able to frustrate Him.

His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺШіЩҺЩ„Ш§Щ…ЩҢ Ш№ЩҺЩ„ЩҺЩү Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШұЩ’ШіЩҺЩ„ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "And peace be to the apostles!" (37:181). This is a salutation upon all the messengers and a protection for them from anything reaching them from Him, the Exalted, that would grieve them or that they would dislike.

His saying, the Exalted: **ЩҲЩҺШ§Щ„Щ’ШӯЩҺЩ…Щ’ШҜЩҸ Щ„ЩҗЩ„Щ‘ЩҺЩҮЩҗ ШұЩҺШЁЩ‘Щҗ Ш§Щ„Щ’Ш№Ш§Щ„ЩҺЩ…ЩҗЩҠЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "And all praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds." (37:182). The discussion regarding this has already passed in the commentary on Surah al-Fatihah.

## (Narrative Discussion)

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*, Muhammad ibn Nadr and Ibn 'Asakir narrated from al-'Ala' ibn Sa'id: that the Messenger of Allah (ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ) said one day to those sitting with him: **ШЈШ·ШӘ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ…Ш§ШЎ ЩҲШӯЩӮ Щ„ЩҮШ§ ШЈЩҶ ШӘШҰШ· ШҢ Щ„ЩҠШі Щ…ЩҶЩҮШ§ Щ…ЩҲШ¶Ш№ ЩӮШҜЩ… ШҘЩ„Ш§ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ Щ…Щ„Щғ ШұШ§ЩғШ№ ШЈЩҲ ШіШ§Ш¬ШҜ** вҖ” "The heaven has groaned, and it has the right to groan; there is not a foot's space in it except that there is an angel bowing or prostrating upon it." Then he recited: **ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҶЩҺШӯЩ’ЩҶЩҸ Ш§Щ„ШөЩ‘ЩҺШ§ЩҒЩ‘ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҶЩҺШӯЩ’ЩҶЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШіЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗШӯЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ** вҖ” "Indeed we are those who stand in ranks. Indeed we are those who celebrate [His] glory."

I say: This meaning has been narrated from him (ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ) through other chains as well.

In the same source, Ibn Mardawayh narrated from Anas: that the Prophet (ШөЩ„ЩүвҖҢШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҢШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҢЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ), when he stood for prayer, would say: **Ш§ШіШӘЩҲЩҲШ§ ШӘЩӮШҜЩ… ЩҠШ§ ЩҒЩ„Ш§ЩҶ ШӘШЈШ®Шұ ЩҠШ§ ЩҒЩ„Ш§ЩҶ ШЈЩӮЩҠЩ…ЩҲШ§ ШөЩҒЩҲЩҒЩғЩ… ЩҖ ЩҠШұЩҠШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЁЩғЩ… ЩҮШҜЩү Ш§Щ„Щ…Щ„Ш§ШҰЩғШ©** вҖ” "Stand straight; move forward, O so-and-so; move back, O so-and-so; straighten your ranksвҖ”Allah intends for you the guidance of the angels." Then he would recite: **ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҶЩҺШӯЩ’ЩҶЩҸ Ш§Щ„ШөЩ‘ЩҺШ§ЩҒЩ‘ЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ ЩҲЩҺШҘЩҗЩҶЩ‘ЩҺШ§ Щ„ЩҺЩҶЩҺШӯЩ’ЩҶЩҸ Ш§Щ„Щ’Щ…ЩҸШіЩҺШЁЩ‘ЩҗШӯЩҸЩҲЩҶЩҺ**.

In *Nahj al-Balaghah*, he (peace be upon him) said in describing the angels: **ЩҲШөШ§ЩҒЩҲЩҶ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠШӘШІШ§ЩҠЩ„ЩҲЩҶ ЩҲЩ…ШіШЁШӯЩҲЩҶ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠШіШЈЩ…ЩҲЩҶ** вҖ” "And they are those who stand in ranks and do not depart, and those who celebrate [His] glory and do not weary."
